reasoning:
The probability rests entirely on MLB (Yankees, Mets), MLS (Red Bulls, NYCFC), and marginally NFL (Giants, Jets), as the 2025 NHL and NBA championships were played without NY teams. 

1. NHL and NBA (Eliminated): The Florida Panthers won the 2025 Stanley Cup. The Oklahoma City Thunder and Indiana Pacers are in the 2025 NBA Finals.
2. NFL (<1% chance): The Jets and Giants are both tied for the worst odds to win Super Bowl 60 (+23000), giving them a combined chance of roughly 0.5%.
3. MLB (~16-18% chance): The Yankees and Mets are the primary carriers of this market. After the Mets signed Juan Soto, their odds improved while the Yankees' lengthened slightly, but both remain solid contenders around +900 to +1100 (approx. 8-10% implied odds each). Removing the sportsbook vig leaves them with a combined ~15-18% true chance of winning the World Series. The Dodgers are heavy favorites. 
4. MLS (~6-8% chance): The Red Bulls reached the 2024 MLS Cup (losing to LA Galaxy), and both NYCFC and Red Bulls are competitive. Inter Miami is the heavy favorite for 2025, but the two NY teams have two paths (MLS Cup + Leagues Cup) which gives them a credible, albeit small, combined chance to win one of those trophies. 

Summing these independent probabilities (MLB ~17% + MLS ~7% + NFL <1%) yields a baseline around 23-25%. This perfectly aligns with the most recent Manifold estimate of 25% from mid-June. I will forecast 23% to slightly adjust for standard overvaluation of large-market teams in betting odds.
